description: Neighbours repairing a roof may call their sustained, purposeful effort 'riola'. The verb treats labour as contribution to something beyond the self, whether the work is done alone or together. 'riporu' (task) is one bounded unit, while module word 'rimawu' (drudgery) records work experienced as depleting and denied meaning.
articulatory_notes: Begin with a quick tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge for 'r', then move into high-front unrounded 'i'. Hold the following stressed middle-height rounded 'o' as a separate syllable across the 'i.o' hiatus. For 'la', form a lateral at the ridge and let the voice pass along the tongue's sides before open central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: A tap launches the word across a wide hiatus; the liquid ending lets it keep going. The contour feels sustained rather than hurried, like work whose meaning appears through continuation.
usage_notes: The verb may stand without an object when the work is understood. Noun position gives purposeful labour or work. Coerced or meaningless activity is not 'riola' when that choice would erase the worker's own account.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Repair days, food growing, and care for shared systems depend on effort offered towards a common future. 'riola' keeps that contribution visible without turning every task into an institution.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Shared labour joins practical skill to the continued life of a household or community. The form fits work whose meaning can still be seen in what it tends or leaves behind.

semantic_domains.activity: sustained purposeful work rather than one bounded task
semantic_domains.community: effort understood as contribution beyond the worker alone
